lines: All done. Here's what I found and did:

**CLAUDE.md** ✅ — Readable. The Sherpa repo is a pnpm monorepo for the behavioral agentic collaboration framework. Notably, Luna (OpenClaw) is already wired in as one of 9 dispatch backends (`backend: openclaw` via WebSocket protocol v3).
**Git** ✅ — Was on `main`, created and switched to `test/luna-repo-access`.
**Commit** ✅ — `947f8da test: verify Luna repo access from OpenClaw` — visible in git log on the new branch.
**Read/write access** ✅ — Full access confirmed. The report is at `docs/tasks/logs/openclaw-repo-test-report.md`.
